image.shortdescription: a numberline with a shaded region; the shaded region extends all the way to the left with an arrowhead; it extends to the right up to 1 where there is a right parenthesis

solution.p: The interval represents all numbers less than 1\text{.} So in set-builder notation, the interval is {\{ x \mid x \lt 1 \}}\text{.}

And we read the number line from left to right, with the shading extending to -\infty on the left and going all the way up to 1 but not including 1\text{.} So in interval notation, the interval is {\left(-\infty ,1\right)}\text{.}
